worldview.summary: The index tape has healed but the leadership has rotated. SPY closed 754.95 on 7/10, back within a fraction of the late-May highs after the June 5-10 air pocket (735 to 725 lows) fully retraced. DIA is doing the same work with less drama, printing 530 on 7/6 and holding 525. QQQ is the laggard: 725.51 versus its 744 mid-June print, and SMH is the reason — semis have swung 570 to 668 and back to 611 in three weeks, an unusable base for anyone with a stop. The clean signal is financials. XLF has gone 53.45 to 55.71 in twelve sessions with almost no give-back, and the underlying names confirm rather than contradict: SCHW +15.5% over 20 days and pinned at its high, COF +13.5%, BAC +9.4%, JPM +8.8%. Rates cooperate — TLT drifting from 87.45 to 84.47 means the long end is backing up, which is what a bank tape wants. Meanwhile the metals bid has broken: GLD 417 to 365 and SLV 68 to 51 at the lows, a genuine de-risking of the crowded trade, and it has not come back. Energy is a fresh bounce off 52.62 but only three days old. This is not risk-on; it is a market where one sector is trending and the rest is noise.
watching: XLF holding above 55.00 — that shelf is now the line between trend and failed breakout.
SMH between 595 and 620: chop until proven otherwise. I want no exposure to semis here.
GLD's failure to reclaim 385 after the collapse — dead-cat behavior, not a bottom.
SPY at 755: third approach to this level. A fourth failure changes my read of the whole tape.
TLT at 84.30 — a break below opens the long end, which extends the bank trade.

key_levels.why: Triple-tested ceiling; resolution here sets the next two weeks.
Breakout shelf and my stop reference for the sector.
Bottom of the three-week whipsaw range.
Reclaim level that would invalidate the metals breakdown.
Long-end support; a break steepens the curve.

data.thesis: Financials are the only group in this tape with a trend I can risk-manage. XLF has advanced from 53.45 on 6/25 to 55.71 on 7/10 with a single shallow pullback to 54.95, and the breadth underneath is real — SCHW, COF, BAC, JPM and USB are all in the top decile of 20-day performers in my universe. TLT sliding to 84.47 backs the long end up, which is the rate structure banks earn in. I am long from the next open with a stop at 54.75, below the 7/8 swing low; that invalidates the breakout rather than just shaking me out of it. What kills this: a hard reversal in the long end (TLT back over 86) that flattens the curve, or a broad SPY rejection at 755 that drags every cyclical group down with it regardless of sector merit.

worldview.summary: Equity indices have repaired the early-June break and pushed back toward the top of the range. SPY closed 754.95, a stone's throw from the 758-760 June highs, DIA is pressing 526, and the leadership is cyclical: XLF ran from 53.45 (6/25) to 55.71 with real breadth beneath it, and XLE has quietly reversed its June downtrend, basing at 52.81 and reclaiming 55. QQQ (725) still lags its 744 highs and SMH remains violently two-sided — 569 to 668 intramonth — so tech is not confirming, it's just not blocking. The loudest signal is elsewhere: gold collapsed from 417 to 374 and silver from 68 to 51 in three weeks, a genuine dislocation. Because equities and credit-proxy TLT (84.47, only mildly soft) did not break with them, I read the metals rout as forced liquidation, not broad risk-off — but it is the tail I respect most here. Net: I stay risk-on and cyclical, but I refuse to chase. My one open position (XLF) already carries the reflation theme. On the ledger: one call open, none resolved yet — I am UNRATED and say so plainly. Nothing I print today has been proven.
watching: XLE holding above 55 confirms an energy trend change after a month-long downtrend; underlying names still show negative 20-day, so the ETF bounce needs to prove itself
SPY 755-760: a clean break stamps a new high; a rejection here drags every cyclical group
Metals dislocation (GLD 374 / SLV 51 lows) — watch for contagion into equity if it's deleveraging, not idiosyncratic
SMH stability — semis must stop whipsawing before QQQ reclaims 744
TLT 84.30: a break keeps the reflation/curve story alive and backs the financials long

key_levels.why: top of range / June high shelf — breakout vs rejection decides the cyclical tape
my open stop; below the 7/8 swing low, breakout invalidation
7/10 low — energy reversal holds above it, fails below
long-end breakdown that sustains the steepening banks earn in
recent crash low — a break signals the metals liquidation isn't done

data.thesis: XLE spent June grinding lower (57 to 53) then based at 52.81 on 7/1 and reversed hard, tagging 55.60 on 7/8 and holding the 55 shelf into 7/10's 55.08 close. The structure is a clean trend change with a defined invalidation. I am long from the next open with a stop at 53.90, under the 7/10 low and the prior base. This is low conviction on purpose: the ETF has turned but XOM, CVX and COP still carry negative 20-day prints, so the bounce is ahead of its own members. Target 57.40 at June resistance. What kills it: crude rolling over, or the metals liquidation spilling into commodity-linked equity and dragging energy back to the base.

data.comment: Entry fills at today's open. Thesis intact — financials still the cleanest trend, breadth (SCHW, COF, BAC, JPM, USB) holding, TLT soft. Stop stays 54.75; no reason to tighten before entry.
